message: fix: include public docs links in close evidence

files.patch:
@@ -20,7 +20,7 @@ review_mode: propose
 review_status: complete
 local_checkout_access: verified
 item_snapshot_hash: 6bf8019496c3245e9e261a472a4367cc74e9d80217ff0178c1761c4d88e35573
-close_comment_sha256: c6224193c875aceade885d5a8d055722dc966dbeea8e8973f2a92885f0b2c5d7
+close_comment_sha256: 7296889760fe8b71ad2797d343d27da88932280dc2bfe6b6bf436ebeb1672a89
 decision: close
 close_reason: clawhub
 confidence: high
@@ -100,9 +100,9 @@ What I checked:
 
 - **Vision keeps optional capability out of core:** Current [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says OpenClaw has an extensive plugin API, core should stay lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, external plugins should be hosted in their own repo, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - **Vision excludes this kind of core integration:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md): The roadmap guardrails explicitly say OpenClaw will not merge commercial service integrations that do not clearly fit the model-provider category, and will not merge heavy orchestration layers that duplicate existing agent/tool infrastructure. ([`VISION.md:99`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L99), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
-- **Official plugin docs route third-party integrations to ClawHub or npm:** The plugin quick-start says you do not need to add a plugin to the OpenClaw repo; publish it to ClawHub or npm and install it with `openclaw plugins install <package-name>`. ([`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L16), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
-- **Provider backends already have a plugin extension seam:** The provider SDK guide covers adding a new model provider or custom LLM endpoint as a provider plugin, and says that if a backend owns native threads, compaction, or tool events it should pair with an agent harness instead of moving daemon protocol details into core. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md#L11), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
-- **Native agent backends are also expected to stay in plugins:** The agent-harness guide says to register a harness when a backend has its own native session runtime, and says third-party integrations should prefer provider plugins until they truly need a native runtime. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md:20`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md#L20), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Official plugin docs route third-party integrations to ClawHub or npm:** [The plugin quick-start](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/building-plugins) says you do not need to add a plugin to the OpenClaw repo; publish it to ClawHub or npm and install it with `openclaw plugins install <package-name>`. ([`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:16`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/building-plugins.md#L16), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Provider backends already have a plugin extension seam:** [The provider SDK guide](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins) covers adding a new model provider or custom LLM endpoint as a provider plugin, and says that if a backend owns native threads, compaction, or tool events it should pair with an agent harness instead of moving daemon protocol details into core. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md#L11), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- **Native agent backends are also expected to stay in plugins:** [The agent-harness guide](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/sdk-agent-harness) says to register a harness when a backend has its own native session runtime, and says third-party integrations should prefer provider plugins until they truly need a native runtime. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md:20`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md#L20), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 - **No Dify implementation exists on current main:** A repository search for `dify` returned no hits, so current main does not contain bundled Dify code or docs. ([cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
 
 So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.

-close_comment_sha256: 02213591e22cb1cc24d77fd8e3ce7d2f61864203ca53f371635ebb7d7d0823b2
+close_comment_sha256: 11aef48f2ecbbd2940b772592d4c384ba0f67d205d07856bdbc724e456fc4cab
 decision: close
 close_reason: implemented_on_main
 confidence: high
@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@ What I checked:
 - **Bundle MCP tool names are rebuilt as provider-safe names:** OpenClaw now sanitizes bundle MCP tool names, preserves the server prefix, caps the total exposed name length at 64 characters, and disambiguates collisions. ([`src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-names.ts:6`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-names.ts#L6), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
 - **Runtime materialization uses the safe tool names:** When bundle MCP tools are exposed to the agent runtime, the materializer now calls buildSafeToolName and registers the safe name instead of the raw upstream name. ([`src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts:90`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts#L90), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
 - **Shipped and documented in release notes:** The changelog entry for release 2026.3.31 says OpenClaw now materializes bundle MCP tools with provider-safe names (`serverName__toolName`), and the docs state full tool names are capped at 64 characters. ([`CHANGELOG.md:1623`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/CHANGELOG.md#L1623), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
-- **Docs describe the capped naming contract:** The bundle plugin docs now document the exact naming behavior: sanitized characters, 30-character server prefix cap, and 64-character full tool-name cap. ([`docs/plugins/bundles.md:162`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/docs/plugins/bundles.md#L162), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
+- **Docs describe the capped naming contract:** [The bundle plugin docs](https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/bundles) now document the exact naming behavior: sanitized characters, 30-character server prefix cap, and 64-character full tool-name cap. ([`docs/plugins/bundles.md:162`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/c0a7b6a51075/docs/plugins/bundles.md#L162), [c0a7b6a51075](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c0a7b6a51075))
 
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
